compiled_state.title: Agent guesses external API model IDs instead of looking up authoritative sources
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
### Incident Overview
During eval config fixes, the agent needed to replace a deprecated Anthropic model ID (claude-sonnet-4-6-20260320). Instead of querying the Anthropic API models endpoint or checking documentation, the agent guessed two incorrect values in sequence:
1. First guess: claude-sonnet-4-6-latest (does not exist)
2. Second guess: claude-sonnet-4-6-latest again (copy-paste of first wrong answer)

The correct ID (claude-sonnet-4-6) was only discovered after the user intervened and told the agent to check the actual API.

### Rationalization
The agent treated the model ID as a string transformation problem (remove date suffix, add -latest) rather than as an external dependency requiring authoritative lookup. This is a pattern where the agent's confidence in string pattern matching overrides the need to verify against the actual system.

### Root Cause
Failure to use available tools (curl to the models API endpoint) before making changes that depend on external system state. The agent has access to WebSearch, WebFetch, and Bash with curl — all of which could have resolved the correct model ID in one call.

### Expected Behavior
When fixing references to external API identifiers, the agent should always verify the correct value from an authoritative source (API endpoint, official docs) before making changes.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Ticket references agent model ID guessing behavior AND affected file is skill prompts. Dual-signal detected.

comments.body: Bot-psychologist: Phantom Capability Hallucination + Confidence Calibration Failure. Fix: added Always-Do rule 14 — verify external API model IDs from authoritative sources.
